question.text: Under SEBI (Credit Rating Agencies) Regulations, 1999, which of the following is a mandatory obligation for CRAs during the rating process?

answer.text: Publish detailed financial projections of the rated entity
Meet the audit committee of the rated entity at least once a year to discuss issues including related party transactions and internal financial controls
Obtain prior consent from the stock exchange before assigning any rating
Submit all rating decisions to SEBI for approval before publication

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI has mandated that CRAs shall meet the audit committee of the rated entity at least once a year to discuss issues including related party transactions, internal financial controls, and other material disclosures made by the management having a bearing on the rating of listed NCDs. **The Trap:** CRAs are not required to submit ratings to SEBI for pre-approval -- ratings are independent decisions of the rating committee. **Cross-Exam Context:** CRA due diligence obligations are tested in NISM Series III-A and NISM Series IX exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Minutes of meetings with issuer management must be recorded and incorporated in the rating committee note -- maintaining documentation is critical.
